subTitle: The Bear Whisperer keeps the peace in the resort town Mammoth Lakes, California where tourists and bears collide.

subTitle: Tiger sharks in the Bahamas; relationship between sharks and remora fish.

title: Second Chance Pets

subTitle: A dog named Draper suffers from amniotic band syndrome; a pit bull needs a knee brace after getting hurt playing.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: Rosie the pug is fitted with a prosthetic after being adopted from China; a dog named Bailey gets a brace.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Rod and Jessie are out of options with their aggressive Australian Cattle Dog, Cooper, whose animal instincts are causing him to herd humans, not cattle; the Dog Dudes must turn this cattle dog's aggressive behavior around.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Aria and Brixton are both fun-loving and friendly, until a traumatic Husky attack leaves Aria with a fear-aggression toward strange dogs; it's up to the Dog Dudes and their pack of Huskies to prove to Aria that Huskies aren't so bad after all.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Dog trainer brothers Dylan and Darijan boast seven years and hundreds of rehabilitated dogs under their collars; Dylan focuses on the dog, Darijan on the human psychology.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: At Mexico's Guadalupe Island, guests come face to face with great white sharks; in the Bahamas, scuba divers pay big bucks to get up close and personal with tiger sharks; shark advocates Dr. Erich Ritter and Chuck Anderson have grave reservations.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: Humpback whales may be in danger again as whaling nations explore new ways to re-open the hunt.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: A look at the marine mammals Manatees and Dugongs; each year, hundreds of these gentle giants' are killed in American waters by boats, disease and cold weather.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Lionfish, a deadly Indo-Pacific species, have invaded the Caribbean and Atlantic, thanks to aquarium fish being released into the sea.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Bear the labrador has a sore leg, which turns into a really expensive problem; Mark travels down to Rotorua to help Stacey with a busy day on the farm.

subTitle: Mark tries to save a girl's pet lamb; Stacey treats Max who has bad arthritis; Mark and Stacey volunteer for the day at SPCA.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Howdie and Dawn are working dogs and their owner wants them to mate, but neither are interested so Stacey intervenes; Mark treats a horse with a very sore leg; Stacey operates on Sushi the cat who has skin cancer.

title: Modern Migrations

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: With a high reproduction rate that leads to large numbers on reserves and are the staple diet for predators that keep their numbers in check; seeing how these highly adapted and unique animals get loaded and gently treated to migrate to a new home.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: Another member of Africa's Big Five, the white rhino; once on the brink of extinction, today thanks to the conservation and breeding strategies, there are enough to relocate to other nature reserves to ensure the survival of this unique species.

subTitle: Four gorillas' emotional journey from South Africa into the Limbe Wildlife Centre in Cameroon.

subTitle: Indonesia's understaffed patrols from the Ministry of Maritime Affairs and Fisheries are having a hard time fighting the syndicate behind the extensive poaching.

subTitle: The attempt to regrow a part of the African wilderness called the karoo.

subTitle: Anchorage has a rather unique resident who is renowned as nature's lumberjack and North Americas largest rodent, the beaver.

subTitle: Anchorage has a resident who is renowned as nature's lumberjack and North America's largest rodent, the beaver; with their powerful teeth, the trees they fell can be huge and their dams can be seen from space.

title: Wild About
subTitle: With only a handful of snakes potentially dangerous to humans and most targeting rodents and pests, it seems their negative reputation today is unearned; a product of their strange limb-less appearance, rather than their nature.

subTitle: Many of the larger breeds of raptors are now endangered or vulnerable, particularly eagles, vultures and larger owls as they require large, specialized habitats; though they may be soaring high now, the future doesn't bode well for the great raptors.

title: Wild About
subTitle: All great apes are highly endangered partly due to hunting and illegal pet trade, but mainly due to shrinking habitats; sitting in the tree tops and jungles is a reminder of our past and our responsibilities for the future.
